blurb: Bright lupine-like spikes of golden pea flowers light up late spring over clean blue-green trifoliate foliage — a vigorous, deer-proof native that spreads into bold drifts.
water: Low to moderate; xeric once established
seasons: Golden flower spikes late spring–early summer; blue-green trifoliate foliage; curved seed pods in summer
wildlife: Bumblebees and native bees; larval host for sulphur butterflies
toxic: Foliage and seeds contain quinolizidine alkaloids — toxic if eaten; keep from children, pets, and livestock.
